textSpans.text: The patient is a 57 year old female who presents for a follow-up visit.
Patient reports the shoulder feels much better as well as the ulnar shaft fractures.
General appearance: The patient is awake, alert and oriented x4.
The patient is in no apparent distress.
Physical examination: On examination of this patient's right shoulder finds no obvious gross deformity.
The patient is grossly neurovascularly intact.
Radiographic examination: X-rays of this patient's right shoulder 2 views finds a mildly impacted and comminuted proximal humerus
Patient will follow-up in 6 weeks for recheck.
All questions were answered to the patient's satisfaction.
The patient will follow up in clinic with myself in 6 weeks for recheck
The patient knows to call and/or return to clinic with any questions and/or concerns
The patient was seen today and examined by PA Zarn and discussed fully with Dr.
DESCRIPTION OF PROCEDURE: The patient was identified in the preoperative holding area where
the correct patient, laterality, and procedure were confirmed.
The patient was brought to the operative suite
time-out was performed to verify the correct patient, laterality, and procedure.
patient tolerated the procedure well and sustained no intraoperative complications.
Unspecified fracture of upper end of right humerus, subsequent encounter for fracture with routine healing ( S42.201D )
Displaced oblique fracture of shaft of right ulna, subsequent encounter for closed fracture with routine healing ( S52.231D)
Unspecified fracture of upper end of right humerus, subsequent encounter for fracture with routine healing ( S42.201D)
S42.201D ) Displaced oblique fracture of shaft of right ulna, subsequent encounter for closed fracture with routine healing ( S52
Good motion of the forearm without pain..
No pain to palpation to the forearm or the
wrist capsule globally, and attritional rupture of
In order to remove this, I would have to remove the capsule and I
A sterile dressing was applied followed by resting hand splint in a protected position.
EXTREMITY ROM, Strengthening, 2XWK FOR 6-8WKS ADD STRETCHING AND GAIT TRAINING

textSpans.text: PT: Eval & Treat w/ Modalities Frequency/Duration: HOME HEALTH EVAL AND TREAT: SKILLED NURSING: HOME PT RT UPPER

Alcohol Assessment: No Current Alcohol Problem 02/01/2024
Current Rehab Status: No Current Drug Rehab 02/01/2024
Current work status: Not Currently Employed 02/01/2024
Drug Assessment: No Ilicit Drug Use 02/01/2024
Past Rehab Status: No Past Drug Rehab 02/01/2024
Surgical Blood Transfusion: Approves surgical blood transfusion 02/01/2024
Displaced oblique fracture of shaft of right ulna, subsequent encounter for closed fracture with routine healing
History of present illness: Very pleasant 57-year-old female with history significant for severe rheumatoid arthritis, osteoporosis and
end-stage right shoulder arthritis presents to my office for reevaluation of her right shoulder and right ulnar shaft fracture.
notes the date of injury for the shoulder fracture was 6 weeks ago.
Date of injury for the ulnar shaft fracture was approximately 5 to
postinjury swelling and ecchymosis to both the shoulder and the forearm. 2+ RP.
Stiff range of motion of the elbow and shoulder albeit improved from prior visit.
Good motion of the wrist and phalanges.
fracture with good congruency of the GH joint.
End-stage arthritis is present of the GH joint.
Interval bony healing seen. 2 views of
the right forearm demonstrates healed ulnar shaft fracture with serial imaging.
Very pleasant 57-year-old female has acute right shoulder proximal humerus fracture with comminution and mild impaction and right
forearm ulnar shaft fracture, stable and improved.
The ulna shaft fracture appears to be healed with repeat imaging.
She may resume normal daily activities.
Patient does voice understanding and agrees with the above plan of care.
Holman Chan, along with plan collaboration
Order 2 views right shoulder next visit
Thank you for allowing me to participate in the care of Mrs.
The dictated information, contained herein, was transcribed with voice recognition software.
Shoulder X-Ray, 2 Views (Taken and Read) - RT
Forearm X-Ray, 2 Views (Taken and Read) - RT
Left ring finger EDC tendon attritional tear.
Left small finger EDC tendon attritional tear.
Left ring finger EDC tendon repair.
Left small finger EDC tendon repair.
Left volar wrist mass, left index, middle, ring, and
small finger inflamed tenosynovitis with rice type
bodies encompassing all tendons; inflamed dorsal
the ring and small extensor tendon.
The risks and benefits of surgery were
discussed and I marked the left wrist with my initials.
where she was administered general anesthesia.
A non-sterile tourniquet was placed on the left upper
The left upper extremity was prepped and draped in a standard sterile fashion.
An Esmarch was used and the tourniquet was inflated to 230 mmHg.
I started with the volar mass.
made a longitudinal incision along the FCR and then made another incision along the plane of the thenar
I spread and identified several small arterial
branches which were cauterized and mobilized.
I traced it down to the radiocarpal
I cauterized it at the stalk and sent it as a specimen to Pathology.
I irrigated the area with curette and used a 3-0 Vicryl suture to sew up the hole.
completed my left volar mass excision.
Then, I turned my attention to the dorsal wrist/hand.
There was a large swelling and protuberance over
I made a large longitudinal incision over the hand.
There was large protuberant mass which was encompassing all the extensor tendons of the fourth
I isolated the tendons initially to the index, middle, ring, and small.
tendons were completely ruptured in an attritional manner with small fibrous bands bridging the tendon.
The mass was very inflamed, protuberant tenosynovitis with gross rice bodies.
debulked and removed as possible, I completed the tenosynovectomy of the index, middle, ring, and small
This was sent as a composite specimen to Pathology.
The index and middle finger tendons were
The ring and small fingers were not.
I resected the bridging synovial bands.
trimmed and freshened up the edges of the ring and small finger tendons.
suture and did a four-portion repair to repair the remaining tendons back to one another.
did not feel safe or comfortable doing this.
At this point, I determined I had to remove as much of the
mass in the form of tenosynovitis as I could safely do with this approach.
Hemostasis was achieved using bipolar electrocautery.
Copious amounts of sterile saline were used to
Then, 3-0 Vicryl was used for dermal layer followed by 4-0 nylon horizontal mattress
Then, 0.25% Marcaine with epinephrine was administered for peri-incisional
to PACU in a stable condition.
